node.title: Uber | Phone Screen | Run async graph

topic.title: Uber | Phone Screen | Run async graph

post.content: I got this question in Uber phone screen recently. Interviewer was very vague in the problem description, just mentioned dependent tasks where I had to design the interface and shape of the tasks. I used topological sort, but the challenge was to execute dependent tasks like D & E, which I struggled with. Needless to say I failed the interview. I asked the interviewer at the end and they mentioned I was on the right track, using topological sort and then finding dependent tasks like A, B -> D and C, D -> E, but I\'m not sure how to do that? I guess once we get that going something like Promise.all(A, B, C).then(D).then(E) should work?

As in all similar problems for scheduling, we need to find a valid execution order and then simply run it. The execution order is a topological sort problem. I think this is what they wanted you to do.

It’s a very easy problem.

Promisify the tasks. E.g.  const taskPromise = new Promise(taskA);

Then use topological sort for order.
Each disjoint set will have it’s own execution path.

Run em all in parallel via Promise.all.
